# ============================================================
# Species distribution classification using the updated island layer
# ============================================================
#
# Main purpose:
# This script overlays BirdLife bird range maps with mainland polygons and
# the updated global island vector. It calculates how much of each species'
# breeding/resident native range overlaps:
#   1. continental mainland
#   2. all checked true islands
#   3. ice-covered true islands
#   4. non-ice true islands
#   5. oceanic true islands
#   6. land-bridge true islands
#
# Input data on the server:
#   BOTW_2025.gpkg
#     BirdLife range maps.
#
#   global_island_filter.gpkg, layer global_islands_filtered
#     Updated island layer.
#     aftercheck == 0 means the polygon is retained as a true island.
#     aftercheck == 1 means the polygon is excluded from island-area analysis.
#     ice separates ice-covered and non-ice islands.
#     category separates oceanic_is and land_bridg islands.
#
#   GlbIslands.gdb, layer Mainlands
#     Mainland polygons used to calculate mainland overlap area.
#
# Output:
#   One CSV per SLURM array job with species-level areas and classification.
#   Additional CSVs contain strict island species, area-failed species,
#   island-mask summaries, and parameter summaries.
#
# Notes:
#   The script runs as a SLURM array. Each task processes a subset of species.
#   All areas are calculated in EPSG:6933, a global equal-area projection.
# ============================================================

library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(data.table)
library(lwgeom)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Spatial processing settings
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# Skip GDAL polygon organization to speed up reading complex polygon data.
Sys.setenv(OGR_ORGANIZE_POLYGONS = "SKIP")

# Use planar geometry because all area calculations are done after projection
# to a global equal-area CRS.
sf_use_s2(FALSE)

# Print warnings immediately. This is useful when checking SLURM log files.
options(warn = 1)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. File paths and output folders
# ------------------------------------------------------------

project_dir <- path.expand("~/island_bird_project")

bird_path <- file.path(project_dir, "BOTW_2025.gpkg")
mainland_gdb <- file.path(project_dir, "GlbIslands.gdb")
island_gpkg <- file.path(project_dir, "global_island_filter.gpkg")
island_layer <- "global_islands_filtered"

out_dir <- file.path(project_dir, "output")
chunk_out_dir <- file.path(out_dir, "global_island_filter_stratified_chunks")

dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
dir.create(chunk_out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# EPSG:6933 = World Cylindrical Equal Area.
# Area values are converted from m2 to km2 after this projection.
equal_area_crs <- 6933

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. BirdLife filters and SLURM array settings
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# BirdLife filters:
# presence = 1 keeps extant ranges.
# origin = 1 keeps native ranges.
# seasonal = 1 and 2 keep resident and breeding ranges.
presence_keep <- c(1)
origin_keep <- c(1)
seasonal_keep <- c(1, 2)

# Small mainland overlaps can come from boundary mismatch or coastline precision.
mainland_tol_km2 <- 1

array_id <- as.integer(Sys.getenv("SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID", "1"))
species_per_job <- as.integer(Sys.getenv("SPECIES_PER_JOB", "100"))
chunk_size <- as.integer(Sys.getenv("CHUNK_SIZE", "10"))

# Calculate which subset of species this array job should process.
offset_n <- (array_id - 1) * species_per_job
job_label <- sprintf("job%03d", array_id)

log_msg <- function(...) {
  message(format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), " | ", ...)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Read BirdLife range polygons for this array job
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# SQL filter for BirdLife range polygons.
bird_where <- paste0(
  "presence IN (", paste(presence_keep, collapse = ","), ") ",
  "AND origin IN (", paste(origin_keep, collapse = ","), ") ",
  "AND seasonal IN (", paste(seasonal_keep, collapse = ","), ")"
)

# Inner query:
#   Selects the species assigned to this SLURM array job.
#
# Outer query:
#   Reads all BirdLife range polygons for those selected species.
bird_query <- paste0("
  SELECT sisid, sci_name, presence, origin, seasonal, geom
  FROM all_species
  WHERE ", bird_where, "
    AND sisid IN (
      SELECT sisid FROM (
        SELECT DISTINCT sisid
        FROM all_species
        WHERE ", bird_where, "
        ORDER BY sisid
        LIMIT ", species_per_job, "
        OFFSET ", offset_n, "
      )
    )
")

log_msg("Reading BirdLife data for ", job_label)

birds_features <- st_read(
  dsn = bird_path,
  query = bird_query,
  quiet = FALSE
)

if (nrow(birds_features) == 0) {
  log_msg("No species assigned to this array job. Exiting.")
  quit(save = "no", status = 0)
}

# Remove empty geometries, repair invalid geometries, and transform to the
# equal-area CRS before calculating any areas.
birds_features <- birds_features[!st_is_empty(birds_features), ] %>%
  st_make_valid() %>%
  st_transform(equal_area_crs)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Dissolve BirdLife polygons by species
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# BirdLife may contain multiple range polygons for the same species.
# This function combines all polygons belonging to one species into one
# species-level geometry.
dissolve_species <- function(x) {
  sid <- x$sisid[1]
  sname <- x$sci_name[1]

  x <- x[!st_is_empty(x), ] %>%
    st_make_valid()

  if (nrow(x) == 0) return(NULL)

# First attempt: dissolve polygons using st_union().
  g <- tryCatch(
    st_union(st_geometry(x)),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

# Fallback: st_buffer(0) sometimes repairs topology problems, then union again.
  if (is.null(g)) {
    x2 <- tryCatch(st_buffer(x, 0), error = function(e) NULL)

    if (!is.null(x2)) {
      g <- tryCatch(
        st_union(st_geometry(st_make_valid(x2))),
        error = function(e) NULL
      )
    }
  }

# Last fallback: combine geometries without dissolving shared boundaries.
  if (is.null(g)) {
    g <- tryCatch(st_combine(st_geometry(x)), error = function(e) NULL)
  }

  if (is.null(g)) return(NULL)

  g <- st_make_valid(g)

# st_make_valid() or st_union() can sometimes return a geometry collection
# containing polygons, lines, or points. Only polygons are useful for area
# calculation, so keep polygon components only.
  if (!inherits(g, "sfc_POLYGON") && !inherits(g, "sfc_MULTIPOLYGON")) {
    g <- st_collection_extract(g, "POLYGON")
  }

  g <- g[!st_is_empty(g)]
  if (length(g) == 0) return(NULL)

# Make sure each species is represented by one geometry object.
  if (length(g) > 1) {
    g <- st_make_valid(st_combine(g))
  }

  st_sf(
    sisid = sid,
    sci_name = sname,
    geometry = g,
    crs = st_crs(x)
  )
}

birds_species <- split(birds_features, birds_features$sisid) %>%
  lapply(dissolve_species) %>%
  Filter(Negate(is.null), .) %>%
  do.call(rbind, .) %>%
  st_make_valid()

# Total range area is calculated after dissolving each species.
birds_species$total_range_area_km2 <- as.numeric(st_area(birds_species)) / 1e6

log_msg("Species after dissolve: ", nrow(birds_species))

rm(birds_features)
gc()

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Read updated island layer and mainland layer helper
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# The updated island layer is stored as a GeoPackage.
# Required columns:
#   aftercheck: 0 = true island used in island-area analysis;
#               1 = excluded polygon.
#   ice:        1 = ice island; 0 = non-ice island.
#   category:   oceanic_is or land_bridg.
read_island_layer <- function(gpkg_path, layer_name) {
  x <- st_read(gpkg_path, layer = layer_name, quiet = FALSE)

  required_cols <- c("aftercheck", "ice", "category")
  missing_cols <- setdiff(required_cols, names(x))

  if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
    stop("Missing required island columns: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
  }

  x %>%
    mutate(
      aftercheck = as.integer(aftercheck),
      ice = as.integer(ice),
      category = as.character(category)
    )
}

# Read geometry only from a geodatabase layer. This is used for mainland
# polygons because their attributes are not needed.
read_geom_only <- function(gdb, layer_name) {
  x <- st_read(gdb, layer = layer_name, quiet = FALSE)
  st_sf(geometry = st_geometry(x), crs = st_crs(x))
}

# Read the island layer, remove empty geometries, repair geometries, and
# transform to the same equal-area CRS as the species ranges.
island_all <- read_island_layer(
  gpkg_path = island_gpkg,
  layer_name = island_layer
) %>%
  .[!st_is_empty(.), ] %>%
  st_make_valid() %>%
  st_transform(equal_area_crs)

# Save a simple polygon count table for checking the island masks.
island_mask_summary <- island_all %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  count(aftercheck, ice, category, name = "n_polygons") %>%
  arrange(aftercheck, ice, category)

log_msg("Island polygons total: ", nrow(island_all))
log_msg("True island polygons aftercheck == 0: ", sum(island_all$aftercheck == 0, na.rm = TRUE))
log_msg("Excluded polygons aftercheck == 1: ", sum(island_all$aftercheck == 1, na.rm = TRUE))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Spatial overlap functions
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# Check whether each species intersects a given spatial mask.
# This function is mostly diagnostic; the area function below also checks
# intersections internally before calculating areas.
intersection_flag <- function(species_sf, mask_sf, flag_name) {
  idx <- split(
    seq_len(nrow(species_sf)),
    ceiling(seq_len(nrow(species_sf)) / chunk_size)
  )

  out <- lapply(seq_along(idx), function(i) {
    log_msg("Checking ", flag_name, " chunk ", i, " / ", length(idx))

    x <- species_sf[idx[[i]], ]

    data.frame(
      sisid = x$sisid,
      flag = lengths(st_intersects(x, mask_sf)) > 0
    )
  }) %>%
    bind_rows()

  names(out)[names(out) == "flag"] <- flag_name
  out
}

# Calculate the overlap area between species ranges and one spatial mask.
# The same function is used for all island masks and mainland polygons.
#
# species_sf:
#   Dissolved species range polygons.
#
# mask_sf:
#   A target polygon layer, such as true islands, ice islands, oceanic islands,
#   land-bridge islands, or mainlands.
#
# area_name and status_name:
#   Output column names, so the same function can produce several area fields.
calc_overlap_area <- function(species_sf, mask_sf, area_name, status_name) {
  base <- species_sf %>%
    st_drop_geometry() %>%
    distinct(sisid)

  zero_rows <- function(ids) {
    data.frame(
      sisid = ids,
      area_km2 = 0,
      area_status = "absent"
    )
  }

  failed_rows <- function(ids) {
    data.frame(
      sisid = ids,
      area_km2 = NA_real_,
      area_status = "area_failed"
    )
  }

  rename_output <- function(out) {
    names(out)[names(out) == "area_km2"] <- area_name
    names(out)[names(out) == "area_status"] <- status_name
    out
  }

# If the mask is empty, every species has zero overlap with this mask.
  if (nrow(mask_sf) == 0) {
    return(rename_output(zero_rows(base$sisid)))
  }

# First find species with any intersection. Species with no intersection are
# assigned zero area later.
  overlap_ids <- base$sisid[
    lengths(st_intersects(species_sf, mask_sf)) > 0
  ]

  xall <- species_sf %>%
    filter(sisid %in% overlap_ids)

  if (nrow(xall) == 0) {
    return(rename_output(zero_rows(base$sisid)))
  }

  idx <- split(
    seq_len(nrow(xall)),
    ceiling(seq_len(nrow(xall)) / chunk_size)
  )

# Calculate geometric intersections in chunks to reduce memory use.
  area_hit <- lapply(seq_along(idx), function(i) {
    log_msg("Calculating ", area_name, " chunk ", i, " / ", length(idx))

    x <- xall[idx[[i]], ]

# Keep only mask polygons that intersect this species chunk. This avoids
# intersecting each species with the full global mask.
    mask_ids <- unique(unlist(st_intersects(x, mask_sf)))

    if (length(mask_ids) == 0) {
      return(zero_rows(x$sisid))
    }

    inter <- tryCatch(
      st_intersection(st_make_valid(x), mask_sf[mask_ids, ]),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )

# If the geometric intersection fails, keep it as NA instead of treating it
# as zero. These rows are reported in the area_failed output.
    if (is.null(inter)) {
      return(failed_rows(x$sisid))
    }

# Keep only polygon overlaps. Boundary-only contacts can produce points or
# lines, but those have no area and should not be counted.
    inter <- st_collection_extract(inter, "POLYGON")
    inter <- inter[!st_is_empty(inter), ]

    if (nrow(inter) == 0) {
      return(zero_rows(x$sisid))
    }

# Convert m2 to km2 and sum all intersected pieces by species.
    area_chunk <- inter %>%
      mutate(area_km2 = as.numeric(st_area(.)) / 1e6) %>%
      st_drop_geometry() %>%
      group_by(sisid) %>%
      summarise(
        area_km2 = sum(area_km2, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      )

    data.frame(sisid = x$sisid) %>%
      left_join(area_chunk, by = "sisid") %>%
      mutate(
        area_km2 = ifelse(is.na(area_km2), 0, area_km2),
        area_status = ifelse(area_km2 > 0, "present", "absent")
      )
  }) %>%
    bind_rows() %>%
# Some species may intersect more than one mask polygon. Summarise again so
# each species has one final area value for this mask.
    group_by(sisid) %>%
    summarise(
      area_km2 = ifelse(
        any(area_status == "area_failed"),
        NA_real_,
        sum(area_km2, na.rm = TRUE)
      ),
      area_status = case_when(
        any(area_status == "area_failed") ~ "area_failed",
        sum(area_km2, na.rm = TRUE) > 0 ~ "present",
        TRUE ~ "absent"
      ),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

# Add back species with no overlap, assigning area = 0 and status = absent.
  out <- base %>%
    left_join(area_hit, by = "sisid") %>%
    mutate(
      area_km2 = ifelse(is.na(area_km2) & is.na(area_status), 0, area_km2),
      area_status = ifelse(is.na(area_status), "absent", area_status)
    )

  rename_output(out)
}

calc_one_mask <- function(mask_sf, area_name, status_name) {
  calc_overlap_area(
    species_sf = birds_species,
    mask_sf = mask_sf,
    area_name = area_name,
    status_name = status_name
  )
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 8. Calculate island overlap areas by mask type
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# true_island_area:
#   All checked true island polygons. This is the main island-area variable.
log_msg("Calculating true island area...")
true_island_area <- island_all %>%
  filter(aftercheck == 0) %>%
  calc_one_mask(
    area_name = "true_island_area_km2",
    status_name = "true_island_area_status"
  )

# ice_island_area:
#   True island polygons that are ice-covered.
log_msg("Calculating ice island area...")
ice_island_area <- island_all %>%
  filter(aftercheck == 0, ice == 1) %>%
  calc_one_mask(
    area_name = "ice_island_area_km2",
    status_name = "ice_island_area_status"
  )

# no_ice_island_area:
#   True island polygons without ice.
log_msg("Calculating no-ice island area...")
no_ice_island_area <- island_all %>%
  filter(aftercheck == 0, ice == 0) %>%
  calc_one_mask(
    area_name = "no_ice_island_area_km2",
    status_name = "no_ice_island_area_status"
  )

# oceanic_island_area:
#   True island polygons classified as oceanic islands.
log_msg("Calculating oceanic island area...")
oceanic_island_area <- island_all %>%
  filter(aftercheck == 0, category == "oceanic_is") %>%
  calc_one_mask(
    area_name = "oceanic_island_area_km2",
    status_name = "oceanic_island_area_status"
  )

# landbridge_island_area:
#   True island polygons classified as land-bridge islands.
log_msg("Calculating landbridge island area...")
landbridge_island_area <- island_all %>%
  filter(aftercheck == 0, category == "land_bridg") %>%
  calc_one_mask(
    area_name = "landbridge_island_area_km2",
    status_name = "landbridge_island_area_status"
  )

rm(island_all)
gc()

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 9. Calculate mainland overlap area
# ------------------------------------------------------------

log_msg("Reading mainland polygons...")
mainlands <- read_geom_only(mainland_gdb, "Mainlands") %>%
  .[!st_is_empty(.), ] %>%
  st_make_valid() %>%
  st_transform(equal_area_crs)

log_msg("Calculating mainland area...")
mainland_area <- calc_overlap_area(
  species_sf = birds_species,
  mask_sf = mainlands,
  area_name = "mainland_area_km2",
  status_name = "mainland_area_status"
)

rm(mainlands)
gc()

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 10. Build final species-level table
# ------------------------------------------------------------

species_result <- birds_species %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  select(sisid, sci_name, total_range_area_km2) %>%
  left_join(mainland_area, by = "sisid") %>%
  left_join(true_island_area, by = "sisid") %>%
  left_join(ice_island_area, by = "sisid") %>%
  left_join(no_ice_island_area, by = "sisid") %>%
  left_join(oceanic_island_area, by = "sisid") %>%
  left_join(landbridge_island_area, by = "sisid") %>%
  mutate(
# Ocean area is inferred as total range area minus mainland and true-island
# overlap. Small negative values can occur if polygon masks overlap slightly.
    ocean_area_km2 = total_range_area_km2 - mainland_area_km2 - true_island_area_km2,
    land_area_km2 = mainland_area_km2 + true_island_area_km2,

# Proportions relative to total BirdLife range area.
    mainland_prop_total = mainland_area_km2 / total_range_area_km2,
    true_island_prop_total = true_island_area_km2 / total_range_area_km2,
    ocean_prop_total = ocean_area_km2 / total_range_area_km2,

    ice_island_prop_total = ice_island_area_km2 / total_range_area_km2,
    no_ice_island_prop_total = no_ice_island_area_km2 / total_range_area_km2,
    oceanic_island_prop_total = oceanic_island_area_km2 / total_range_area_km2,
    landbridge_island_prop_total = landbridge_island_area_km2 / total_range_area_km2,

# Proportions relative to terrestrial area only.
    mainland_prop_land = ifelse(land_area_km2 > 0, mainland_area_km2 / land_area_km2, NA_real_),
    true_island_prop_land = ifelse(land_area_km2 > 0, true_island_area_km2 / land_area_km2, NA_real_),

# Diagnostic checks. The sums should be close to true_island_area_km2, unless
# categories overlap or geometry precision creates small differences.
    ice_sum_area_km2 = ice_island_area_km2 + no_ice_island_area_km2,
    ice_area_difference_km2 = true_island_area_km2 - ice_sum_area_km2,

    category_sum_area_km2 = oceanic_island_area_km2 + landbridge_island_area_km2,
    category_area_difference_km2 = true_island_area_km2 - category_sum_area_km2,

    any_area_failed = mainland_area_status == "area_failed" |
      true_island_area_status == "area_failed" |
      ice_island_area_status == "area_failed" |
      no_ice_island_area_status == "area_failed" |
      oceanic_island_area_status == "area_failed" |
      landbridge_island_area_status == "area_failed",

# Broad island-only flag using the 1 km2 mainland tolerance.
    island_only_area_based = !any_area_failed &
      true_island_area_km2 > 0 &
      mainland_area_km2 <= mainland_tol_km2,

# Conservative land distribution classes.
# Strict island-endemic:
#   true island area > 0
#   mainland area <= 1 km2
#   >= 99% of terrestrial range on islands
    strict_island_endemic = !any_area_failed &
      true_island_area_km2 > 0 &
      mainland_area_km2 <= mainland_tol_km2 &
      true_island_prop_land >= 0.99,

# Strict mainland species:
#   mainland area > 0
#   true island area <= 1 km2
#   >= 99% of terrestrial range on mainland
    strict_mainland_species = !any_area_failed &
      mainland_area_km2 > 0 &
      true_island_area_km2 <= mainland_tol_km2 &
      mainland_prop_land >= 0.99,

# Island-mainland shared:
#   meaningful area and proportion on both island and mainland.
    island_mainland_shared = !any_area_failed &
      true_island_area_km2 > 1 &
      mainland_area_km2 > 1 &
      true_island_prop_land >= 0.05 &
      mainland_prop_land >= 0.05,

    land_distribution_class = case_when(
      any_area_failed ~ "area_failed",
      strict_island_endemic ~ "Strict island-endemic",
      strict_mainland_species ~ "Strict mainland species",
      island_mainland_shared ~ "Island-mainland shared",
      true_island_prop_land >= 0.95 & mainland_prop_land < 0.05 ~ "Island-dominant",
      mainland_prop_land >= 0.95 & true_island_prop_land < 0.05 ~ "Mainland-dominant",
      land_area_km2 == 0 ~ "No terrestrial overlap",
      TRUE ~ "Ambiguous / check manually"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(land_distribution_class, mainland_area_km2, true_island_area_km2, sci_name)

# Tables for quick checks.
area_failed_species <- species_result %>%
  filter(any_area_failed)

strict_island_species <- species_result %>%
  filter(strict_island_endemic)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 11. Write output tables
# ------------------------------------------------------------

fwrite(
  species_result,
  file.path(chunk_out_dir, paste0("all_species_island_stratified_area_", job_label, ".csv"))
)

fwrite(
  strict_island_species,
  file.path(chunk_out_dir, paste0("strict_island_species_", job_label, ".csv"))
)

fwrite(
  area_failed_species,
  file.path(chunk_out_dir, paste0("area_failed_species_", job_label, ".csv"))
)

fwrite(
  island_mask_summary,
  file.path(chunk_out_dir, paste0("island_mask_summary_", job_label, ".csv"))
)

parameter_summary <- data.frame(
  parameter = c(
    "bird_path",
    "island_gpkg",
    "island_layer",
    "mainland_gdb",
    "presence_keep",
    "origin_keep",
    "seasonal_keep",
    "mainland_tol_km2",
    "equal_area_crs",
    "array_id",
    "species_per_job",
    "offset_n",
    "chunk_size",
    "n_species_analyzed",
    "n_strict_island_species",
    "n_area_failed",
    "true_island_rule",
    "ice_island_rule",
    "no_ice_island_rule",
    "oceanic_island_rule",
    "landbridge_island_rule"
  ),
  value = c(
    bird_path,
    island_gpkg,
    island_layer,
    mainland_gdb,
    paste(presence_keep, collapse = ","),
    paste(origin_keep, collapse = ","),
    paste(seasonal_keep, collapse = ","),
    mainland_tol_km2,
    equal_area_crs,
    array_id,
    species_per_job,
    offset_n,
    chunk_size,
    nrow(species_result),
    nrow(strict_island_species),
    nrow(area_failed_species),
    "aftercheck == 0",
    "aftercheck == 0 and ice == 1",
    "aftercheck == 0 and ice == 0",
    "aftercheck == 0 and category == oceanic_is",
    "aftercheck == 0 and category == land_bridg"
  )
)

fwrite(
  parameter_summary,
  file.path(chunk_out_dir, paste0("analysis_parameters_", job_label, ".csv"))
)

log_msg("Analysis finished.")
log_msg("Output folder: ", chunk_out_dir)
